purpose: Diagnoses the macro state embedded in cross-asset volatility divergences and identifies likely resolution paths using the SDF inconsistency framework
components: MOVE level (100-120): reflects fiscal issuance uncertainty, QT, BoJ normalization
VIX level (13-18): reflects Kalecki earnings support + vol-selling complex suppression
G10 FX vol (7-9%): reflects carry-trade positioning into rate differentials
SDF inconsistency: cross-asset divergence as violation of single stochastic discount factor
Kalecki fiscal channel as structural explanation for divergence
Resolution probability assessment: VIX-up vs MOVE-down
current_assessment: The divergence is diagnostically meaningful (8/10 confidence) but timing-uncertain. The Kalecki fiscal channel provides a structural explanation that could represent either a transitional state or a novel equilibrium. The non-stationarity paradox (structural microstructure change AND historical analogue validity cannot both be fully true) remains unresolved. Resolution direction is likely VIX-up (6/10) but precision is unjustified by the n=3 evidence base.
limitations: n=3 historical analogues with structurally different drivers
Bayesian posterior on resolution timing has 95% CI statistically indistinguishable from coin flip
Kalecki fiscal channel may represent genuinely novel regime
No mechanism for concluding vol is fairly priced (fragility narrative bias)
